svg.text: SUCCESSFUL MIGRATION TO API v3 OBSERVED IN PRODUCTION
Researchers confirm zero-downtime cutover after 4 sprints of preparation
Principal Investigator: Raj P. | Co-authors: The entire Backend Guild

text.tspan: After extensive laboratory testing spanning four sprint cycles,
the Backend Guild has successfully migrated all 47 endpoints
from API v2 to v3 in production. The migration, initially
hypothesized to require "about two sprints," demonstrated the
well-documented phenomenon of scope expansion under observation.
Key findings include: backward compatibility was maintained for
100% of clients, response times improved by 12% on average,
and only one incident occurred during the cutover - a brief
period where the health check endpoint returned "I'm fine,
everything is fine" instead of a proper 200 response.
Our peer review process shows marked
hours. However, the quality of review
comments has shifted. 42% of all comments
are now "LGTM" or a thumbs up emoji.
January that nobody claimed ownership of.
They've been... dealt with. The pool
config took two attempts - the first set
but every team touched the migration.
fixed a bug in the billing

svg.text: "Good migrations, like good experiments, require patience, documentation,
and the humility to admit when your hypothesis was wrong." - Raj
Published by CloudCo Research Labs | Impact factor: Surprisingly high
Researchers: Raj, Deepa, Nikos, Sam, Wei & the Backend Guild | Lab Director: Raj P. | Submission: Sprint 19
